annotations.label: Second-order logic (as extension of first-order logic)

anchor.snippet: second-order logic is an extension of first-order logic
second-order logic, in addition, quantifies over relations

annotations.label: Law of excluded middle as a second-order sentence

anchor.snippet: says that for every formula P , and every individual x , either Px is true or not( Px ) is true
The domain is a set over which individual elements may be quantified.
we can take an atomic sentence like Cube( b ) and obtain a quantified sentence
is not a sentence of first-order logic, but this is a legitimate sentence of second-order logic
the existence of this set can be asserted in second-order logic as
the set of all cubes and tetrahedrons does not contain any dodecahedrons
every set of people containing y and closed under the Parent relation contains x

annotations.label: No second-order variables for properties of predicates

anchor.snippet: we don't have variables for properties of predicates
Objects are defined to be equal when they share all properties
it can be expressed concisely as a single axiom
A sort of variables that range over sets of individuals.
there is a sort of variables that ranges over all k -ary relations on the individuals
there is a sort of variables that ranges over all functions taking k elements of the domain and returning a single element of the domain
a well-formed formula with no free variables

annotations.label: Function variables reducible to relation variables

anchor.snippet: an n -ary function variable can be represented by a relation variable of arity n +1
Weak second-order logic (WSO) is a restriction of second-order logic in which only quantification over finite sets is allowed
Monadic second-order logic (MSO) is a restriction of second-order logic in which only quantification over unary relations
The second-order logic without these restrictions is sometimes called full second-order logic

annotations.label: Decidability of the MSO theory of the infinite binary tree (S2S)

anchor.snippet: The MSO theory of the complete infinite binary tree ( S2S ) is decidable

annotations.label: Undecidability of full second-order logic over infinite domains

anchor.snippet: can interpret the true second-order arithmetic and is thus undecidable
A formula in second-order logic is said to be of first-order
formula is one additionally having some existential quantifiers over second-order variables
The fragment of second-order logic consisting only of existential second-order formulas is called existential second-order logic
is defined dually, it is called universal second-order logic

annotations.label: Higher fragments defined by mutual recursion

anchor.snippet: More expressive fragments are defined for any k > 0 by mutual recursion
In standard semantics, also called full semantics, the quantifiers range over all sets or functions
A model with this condition is called a full model
Leon Henkin (1950) defined an alternative kind of semantics for second-order and higher-order theories
Henkin proved that Gödel's completeness theorem and compactness theorem

annotations.label: Henkin models as disguised first-order models (Lindström)

anchor.snippet: Lindström's theorem imports that Henkin models are just disguised first-order models

annotations.label: Non-standard interpretations as a consequence of Gödel's incompleteness theorem

anchor.snippet: a necessary consequence of Gödel's incompleteness theorem

annotations.label: Additive inverse expressible in first-order logic

anchor.snippet: one can assert in first-order logic the existence of an additive inverse of each real number
one needs second-order logic to assert the least-upper-bound property for sets of real numbers, which states that every bounded, nonempty set of real numbers has a supremum

annotations.label: Categoricity of ordered fields satisfying the LUB property

anchor.snippet: Any ordered field that satisfies this property is isomorphic to the real number field
Thus the least-upper-bound property cannot be expressed by any set of sentences in first-order logic

annotations.label: Real-closed fields share first-order theory with the reals

anchor.snippet: every real-closed field satisfies the same first-order sentences in the signature
use the sentence that says that every surjective function from the domain to itself is injective

annotations.label: Countable cardinality expressible in second-order logic

anchor.snippet: there is a bijection between every two infinite subsets of the domain
it is not possible to characterize finiteness or countability, respectively, in first-order logic

annotations.label: ESO fragment more expressive than first-order logic

anchor.snippet: Certain fragments of second-order logic like ESO are also more expressive than first-order logic
A deductive system for a logic is a set of inference rules and logical axioms

annotations.label: No deductive system complete for standard semantics

anchor.snippet: none can be complete for the standard semantics

annotations.label: Weakest deductive system for second-order logic

anchor.snippet: The weakest deductive system that can be used consists of a standard deductive system for first-order logic

annotations.label: Soundness of comprehension and choice axioms

anchor.snippet: These axioms are sound for standard second-order semantics

annotations.label: Attempted reduction to a two-sorted first-order theory

anchor.snippet: One might attempt to reduce the second-order theory of the real numbers, with full second-order semantics, to the first-order theory
That theorem implies that there is some countably infinite subset of the real numbers

annotations.label: Internal numbers fail the full least-upper-bound axiom

anchor.snippet: implies that that set does not satisfy the full least-upper-bound axiom

annotations.label: Uniqueness of the Archimedean complete ordered field

anchor.snippet: This follows from the classical theorem that there is only one Archimedean complete ordered field

annotations.label: Second-order theory of the reals not reducible to first-order theory

anchor.snippet: This shows that the second-order theory of the real numbers cannot be reduced to a first-order theory
There is a finite second-order theory whose only model is the real numbers if the continuum hypothesis holds

annotations.label: No sound, complete, effective deductive system for full second-order logic

anchor.snippet: It is a corollary of Gödel's incompleteness theorem that there is no deductive system

annotations.label: Henkin's completeness theorem for Henkin semantics

anchor.snippet: Henkin proved that the standard deductive system for first-order logic is sound, complete, and effective for second-order logic with Henkin semantics

annotations.label: Compactness and Löwenheim–Skolem fail for full models

anchor.snippet: do not hold for full models of second-order logic
REG (the regular languages ) is the set of languages definable by monadic, second-order formulas
NP is the set of languages definable by existential, second-order formulas

annotations.label: co-NP characterized by universal second-order formulas

anchor.snippet: co-NP is the set of languages definable by universal, second-order formulas
PH is the set of languages definable by second-order formulas

annotations.label: PSPACE characterized by second-order formulas with transitive closure

anchor.snippet: PSPACE is the set of languages definable by second-order formulas with an added transitive closure operator

annotations.label: EXPTIME characterized by second-order formulas with least fixed point

anchor.snippet: EXPTIME is the set of languages definable by second-order formulas with an added least fixed point operator

annotations.label: PH = PSPACE consequence for transitive closure expressiveness

anchor.snippet: if PH = PSPACE , then adding a transitive closure operator to second-order logic would not make it any more expressive
